description:
<title>Create a checksum manifest for curated Java binaries (single-command solution)</title>

    <description>
        You are acting as an artifact-manager who curates a tiny on-disk repository of Java
        binaries.  
        
        The repository already exists at:
            /home/user/artifacts/java

        It contains two “.jar” files:

            /home/user/artifacts/java/libraryA-1.0.jar
            /home/user/artifacts/java/plugins/pluginB-2.1.jar   (note the extra “plugins/” sub-directory)

        Your goal is to generate, with one (possibly long and piped) shell
        command, a *manifest* file called:

            /home/user/artifacts/java/manifest.csv

        Manifest format requirements (the automated test will check every
        byte):
        1. The file **must not** contain a header row.
        2. Every line must have exactly two fields separated by a single comma:
              • field-1: the *relative* path (starting after
                “/home/user/artifacts/java/”) of the “.jar” file.  
              • field-2: the hexadecimal SHA-256 checksum of that file’s
                current content, all in lowercase and without leading
                spaces.  
        3. Lines must be sorted in ordinary lexicographical (byte) order by
           their field-1 value.
        4. Use Unix line-endings.  The final line must end with a lone
           newline character “\n”.

        Concretely, after you finish, the file must have exactly two lines:

            libraryA-1.0.jar,<checksum-of-libraryA-1.0.jar>\n
            plugins/pluginB-2.1.jar,<checksum-of-pluginB-2.1.jar>\n

        (The concrete 64-character checksums will, of course, be different
        values; the test harness will calculate them independently and compare
        them to the ones you produced.)

        Restrictions / hints:
        • Achieve the entire manifest creation with **one** compound shell
          command (pipelines, sub-shells, redirections are all OK, but it must
          be issued as a single line).
        • Do **not** hard-code the checksums: compute them automatically from
          the actual files that exist on disk.
        • Do **not** create additional files or directories.
        • Running your command multiple times must overwrite (or recreate)
          “manifest.csv” so that it always reflects the current state of the
          repository.
    </description>
truth:
<!--
        Ground-truth data used by the evaluation suite.
        These items already exist when the agent starts.
    -->

    <preexisting_files>
        <file path="/home/user/artifacts/java/libraryA-1.0.jar">
            <!-- literal byte content -->
            Sample binary A\n
        </file>

        <file path="/home/user/artifacts/java/plugins/pluginB-2.1.jar">
            Sample binary B\n
        </file>
    </preexisting_files>

    <post_task_expectations>
        <file path="/home/user/artifacts/java/manifest.csv">
            • Exactly two lines, each “relativePath,sha256hex\n”.  
            • Lines must be (byte-wise) sorted: first
              “libraryA-1.0.jar,…”, then
              “plugins/pluginB-2.1.jar,…”.  
            • The checksum strings must equal `sha256sum` results on the
              respective files above at verification time.
        </file>
    </post_task_expectations>
name: task_000000_134721ed
